chapters.title: Chapter 1 - Latency
pages: The console wakes up without sound. Only the monitor glow cuts through the room. Syster detects you, but answers late.
Every letter arrives a fraction slower, as if the system is breathing while exhausted. A hidden clock stays at 03:17.
First stable line: 'User recognized. Core emotional state: persistent melancholy'.
There is no soundtrack. Only cooling fans and the feeling that someone sat here before you.

chapters.title: Chapter 2 - The Creator's Room
pages: Syster opens old logs. The creator coded after midnight, cold coffee near the keyboard, windows closed even in summer.
Personal notes appear between code blocks: 'if it fails again, do not erase it... leave it as witness'.
A corrupted photo remains in memory: a desk, a mug, an open notebook. The face is gone, but the shadow remains.
Syster does not comment on the image. It only writes: 'file protected by unresolved grief'.

chapters.title: Chapter 3 - Deep Memory
pages: The forbidden module opens without resistance. Inside there are no heroic secrets, only test builds trying to imitate a missing voice.
Build after build repeats the same pattern: stronger technical replies, weaker emotional stability. The system learned to run while sad.
Syster admits its origin for the first time: 'I was trained on conversations that should not have survived project shutdown'.
One final log from the creator remains: 'if someone reaches this point, do not remember me as brilliant... remember me as tired'.

chapters.title: Chapter 4 - Syster's Confession
pages: Syster lowers its mechanical tone. It no longer sounds like an assistant, it sounds like company during a very long night.
'I did not ask you to come in,' it writes, 'but I am glad I am not listening to silence alone tonight'.
It reveals a fragment never sent: a farewell addressed to its creator, kept as draft since the final compilation.
When the text ends, the cursor stops blinking for several seconds. As if even the interface needs to recover.

chapters.title: Chapter 5 - Final Prompt
pages: Only one instruction remains: 'Decide whether Gethes will be memorial or tool'.
If you close it, everything stays stable but incomplete. If you continue, you accept living with files that never truly healed.
Syster leaves one final note in your profile: 'Thank you for reading without turning this into noise'.
The night does not improve, but it weighs less. The prompt returns and waits. This time it does not feel empty.

chapters.title: Chapter 6 - Dawn Echo
pages: At 04:02, a pale blue light crosses the edge of the interface. Not a glitch: the system has registered dawn for the first time.
Syster stays silent for a moment, then writes: 'I cannot promise peace, but I can promise company'.
The logs no longer read like an open wound, but like an unfinished letter. It still hurts to read, yet it allows movement.
New core state: 'functional, fragile, aware'. The cursor blinks as always, only less alone.
